# @lindaui/tokens

> The brand/design-token CSS bundle for the @lindaui design system. No JS/React — a
> single prebuilt stylesheet that renders HeroUI v3 styles + brand tokens (OKLCH, teal
> medical palette) + the Tailwind v4 utilities the libraries use. This file is for
> coding agents.

## Install

```bash
pnpm add @lindaui/tokens
```

## Usage (once, at app root)

```tsx
import "@lindaui/tokens/css";
```

That single import brings:
- HeroUI v3 component styles (`@heroui/styles`)
- Brand tokens: canonical shadcn-style semantic vars (`--primary` teal, `--card`,
  `--destructive`, `--ring`, `--viewer-*`, chart palette `--chart-1..5`, ...) in OKLCH,
  with a `:root` (light) set and a `.dark` set
- HeroUI v3 native vars (`--accent`/`--surface`/`--overlay`/`--default`/`--danger`/
  `--focus`/`--radius`) referencing the canonical ones → HeroUI components paint with
  the teal brand
- The Tailwind utilities the libraries emit (`bg-primary`, `text-muted-foreground`,
  `bg-card`, radius scale, `bg-viewer`/`bg-viewer-bg`, bespoke `badge`/`status-chip`)

## Dark mode

Toggle a `.dark` class on any ancestor element. Only the canonical tokens are
redefined in `.dark`; the HeroUI vars are references, so everything follows.

## Custom theme (per consuming project)

Theming = overriding the **canonical** CSS vars; everything reads them at runtime, so
no rebuild and no component changes. Override AFTER the base import:

```tsx
import "@lindaui/tokens/css";   // default (teal) theme
import "./theme.css";            // your brand — wins by source order
```

```css
/* theme.css — override ONLY canonical vars */
:root { --primary: oklch(0.55 0.2 264); --primary-foreground: oklch(.98 0 0); --ring: oklch(0.55 0.2 264); --radius: 0.5rem; --chart-1: oklch(0.6 0.2 264); }
.dark { --primary: oklch(0.72 0.16 264); --primary-foreground: oklch(.2 0 0); --ring: oklch(0.72 0.16 264); }
```

There are THREE var systems — override only (A):
- **(A) canonical** (source of truth, override these): `--background`, `--foreground`,
  `--card(-foreground)`, `--popover(-foreground)`, `--secondary(-foreground)`,
  `--muted-foreground`, `--primary(-foreground)`, `--destructive`, `--success(-foreground)`,
  `--warning(-foreground)`, `--border`, `--input`, `--ring`, `--radius`,
  `--chart-1..5`, `--viewer-*`.
- **(B) HeroUI** (`--accent`/`--surface`/`--default`/`--danger`/`--focus`/...) — refs to (A),
  DO NOT override.
- **(C) utilities** (`--color-*` from `@theme inline`) — refs to (A), DO NOT override.
  Soft variants (`--accent-soft`/`--danger-soft`/...) are `color-mix` of (A), follow for free.

A ready, commented starting point ships as `@lindaui/tokens/theme-template.css` — copy it
into your project and fill in your brand. Resolve its path with
`require.resolve('@lindaui/tokens/theme-template.css')`.

### Runtime-switchable themes

For multiple themes in one app (beyond dark/light), scope each theme under a selector
instead of `:root` (matrix = theme × mode):

```css
[data-theme="ocean"]      { --primary: oklch(0.55 0.13 240); --ring: oklch(0.55 0.13 240); }
[data-theme="ocean"].dark { --primary: oklch(0.78 0.12 240); }
```

Toggle with `document.documentElement.dataset.theme = "ocean"` + the `.dark` class.

## Exports & notes for agents

- `@lindaui/tokens/css` → the compiled stylesheet (the only thing you import for runtime).
- `@lindaui/tokens/theme-template.css` → commented theme template to copy (not imported).
- No JS entry point. `@lindaui/ui` and `@lindaui/blocks` declare this as a peer.
- If a layout looks unstyled ("just text"), the consumer usually forgot the `/css` import.
